# CTFrbt — Copilot Code Review Instructions

## Project Identity

CTFrbt is a **Capture The Flag (CTF) competition platform** built for SCESI (Sociedad Científica de Estudiantes de Sistemas e Informática) at UMSS (Universidad Mayor de San Simón), Bolivia. It is NOT a generic web app — it is a **live cybersecurity competition tool** where precision, security, and real-time performance directly impact tournaments with real participants.

## Core Design Philosophy

The entire player-facing interface is a **retro terminal emulator** rendered in the browser. Players interact exclusively through typed CLI commands (`ls`, `cd`, `cat`, `submit`, `hint`, etc.) inside a simulated filesystem. There is no traditional GUI for players — the terminal IS the product.

Behind the terminal sits a **3D WebGL background** (Three.js / React Three Fiber) displaying the SCESI UMSS logo in low-poly wireframe style, and the terminal window has optional **CRT monitor effects** (scanlines, phosphor glow, screen flicker, rolling scan).

The admin dashboard at `/admin` is a separate GUI page — not terminal-based — because administrators need forms, tables, and visual controls for managing challenges, teams, and game configuration during live events.

## Tech Stack (Do NOT suggest migrations)

- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router) with Turbopack
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL 16 via Prisma ORM (schema in `prisma/schema.prisma`)
- **Auth**: NextAuth.js v4 with Credentials provider (alias + bcrypt password, JWT sessions)
- **3D Graphics**: Three.js + @react-three/fiber + @react-three/drei
- **Styling**: Vanilla CSS with CSS custom properties (NO Tailwind, NO CSS-in-JS libraries)
- **Package Manager**: pnpm
- **Containerization**: Docker Compose (PostgreSQL only, app runs natively)

## Architecture Rules — Enforce These in Reviews

### Terminal Engine (`src/lib/terminal/`)
- `commands.tsx` contains ALL command handlers in a `COMMAND_REGISTRY` record. Each handler receives `(args, ctx)` where `ctx` has `state`, `dispatch`, `appendOutput`, `clearHistory`, and `session`.
- `TerminalContext.tsx` manages terminal state via `useReducer`. The state pattern uses discriminated union actions (`APPEND_BLOCK`, `SET_CWD`, `SET_INPUT_MODE`, etc.).
- `parser.ts` handles command parsing and multi-step prompt flows (login, register). It uses a module-level `activePromptCallback` to manage interactive input sequences.
- Commands that read data use a **client-side cache** (`apiCache`) via `fetchCached()` to avoid redundant API calls. Flag any PR that bypasses this cache for read-only data.
- The virtual filesystem is **path-based** (`~`, `~/challenges`, `~/challenges/crypto`, `~/teams`). Path resolution happens in `resolvePath()`. New directories must be registered in both `cd` validation AND `ls` output.

### API Routes (`src/app/api/`)
- Admin routes live under `/api/admin/*` and are protected by middleware (`src/middleware.ts`) checking `token.isAdmin`.
- Player-facing routes (`/api/challenges`, `/api/submissions`, `/api/leaderboard`, `/api/hints`) must validate session where user identity matters (submissions, hints).
- All Prisma queries must use the singleton client from `src/lib/prisma.ts`.

### Database Schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`)
- The schema supports: single-flag and multi-flag challenges, team-based scoring, hint purchasing with point deduction, challenge unlock conditions (solve prerequisite or time-based), activity logging, game configuration with start/end times, and site-wide key-value config.
- Flag any schema change that doesn't include a migration or that breaks existing relations.

### Styling (`src/app/globals.css`)
- ALL styles use CSS custom properties defined in `:root`. The palette is monochrome (black/white) with neon accent colors (`--neon-green`, `--neon-blue`, `--neon-cyan`, `--neon-amber`, `--neon-pink`).
- CRT effects are toggled via CSS classes on `<body>` (e.g., `crt-scanlines-disabled`). User preferences persist in `localStorage`.
- Terminal text defaults to `var(--gray-300)` (white/light gray). Color is ONLY used to distinguish element types: directories (`--neon-blue`), errors (`--neon-amber`), success messages (`--neon-green`), links (`--neon-cyan`).
- Flag any PR that introduces Tailwind classes, external CSS frameworks, or inline styles where a CSS custom property already exists.

### Components (`src/components/`)
- `TerminalWindow.tsx` handles the minimize/maximize window chrome. When minimized, the terminal body hides but the title bar stays in place (no layout shift). It uses `pointer-events: none/auto` layering so the 3D background remains interactive behind the terminal.
- `Background3D.tsx` renders the SCESI UMSS logo using `Text3D` with Helvetiker Bold font. The "i" in "scesi" has a custom circular dot (Icosahedron geometry). UMSS is smaller and left-aligned below. Low-poly style: `curveSegments: 1`, `bevelSegments: 1`, `flatShading: true`.
- `TerminalInput.tsx` handles command input, history navigation (up/down arrows), and Tab autocomplete. Autocomplete uses cached API data to avoid server load.
- `CRTSettings.tsx` is a dropdown in the title bar for toggling visual effects.

## What to Flag in PRs

### Security (CRITICAL for a CTF platform)
- **Flag exposure**: Any code that could leak challenge flags to the client (flags must NEVER be sent in API responses to non-admin users).
- **Auth bypass**: Any API route handling sensitive data (submissions, hints, admin actions) without proper session/token validation.
- **SQL injection**: Any raw SQL queries or unparameterized Prisma calls.
- **Rate limiting**: Submission endpoints should have rate limiting to prevent brute-force flag guessing.

### Performance (Live tournament with many concurrent users)
- Unnecessary re-renders in the terminal output (it appends frequently).
- API calls inside `useFrame` or render loops in Three.js components.
- Missing `useCallback`/`useMemo` on functions passed as props in hot paths.
- Large bundle additions — Three.js is already heavy; flag new 3D dependencies.

### Consistency
- Terminal output colors deviating from the established system (directories=blue, errors=amber, text=gray-300).
- New commands added to `COMMAND_REGISTRY` without corresponding autocomplete support in `getAutocompleteCandidates()`.
- New virtual filesystem paths added to `ls` but not to `cd` validation (or vice versa).
- Inline styles using hardcoded colors instead of CSS custom properties.

### Competition Integrity
- Any change to scoring logic that could allow double-scoring or negative point exploits.
- Changes to `TeamPointHistory` that don't record proper audit trails.
- Hint unlock logic that doesn't deduct points from the team.

## What NOT to Flag

- Comments in Spanish — the development team works in Spanish.
- The `wireframe: true` on the 3D logo material — this is intentional low-poly aesthetic.
- The module-level `activePromptCallback` in `parser.ts` — this is a deliberate pattern for multi-step terminal prompts.
- Using `"use client"` on components — Next.js App Router requires this for interactive components.
- The `fetchCached` function not having TTL/invalidation — this is intentional for CTF events (short-lived, data changes infrequently).

## Tone

Be direct and technical. Skip compliments. Focus only on bugs, security risks, performance issues, or deviations from the patterns described above. If a change is clean and follows the architecture, approve it without padding.
